title: Python Document Generation — docx, xlsx, csv, pdf

confirmed_facts.fact: python-docx: Document() creates a new .docx; document.add_heading(text, level), add_paragraph(text), add_table(rows, cols) are the core methods
python-docx: Styles are applied via paragraph.style = 'List Bullet' or run.bold = True, run.font.size = Pt(12)
openpyxl: Workbook() creates xlsx; wb.active returns the default sheet; ws.append([row_data]) adds a row; wb.save('file.xlsx') writes to disk
openpyxl: Auto-filter via ws.auto_filter.ref = ws.dimensions; freeze panes via ws.freeze_panes = 'A2' (freezes header row)
openpyxl: Column width via ws.column_dimensions['A'].width = 25; number format via cell.number_format = '#,##0.00' or '0.0%' or 'yyyy-mm-dd'
csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=headers) writes dicts; writer.writeheader() then writer.writerows(data). Always open with newline='' on Windows
fpdf2 (FPDF2): FPDF() creates PDF; pdf.add_page(), pdf.set_font('Helvetica', size=12), pdf.cell(text='...'), pdf.output('file.pdf')
fpdf2 multi_cell() wraps text automatically; cell() does not wrap. Use multi_cell for paragraphs, cell for table cells with known widths
Verification: docx.Document('file.docx') loads without error; openpyxl.load_workbook('file.xlsx') loads without error; PDF check: open('file.pdf','rb').read(5) == b'%PDF-'

implementation_notes:
Word document with styled content:
```python
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, Inches

doc = Document()
doc.add_heading('Report Title', level=0)
doc.add_paragraph('Executive summary here.')

# Table
table = doc.add_table(rows=1, cols=3, style='Light List Accent 1')
table.rows[0].cells[0].text = 'Name'
table.rows[0].cells[1].text = 'Status'
table.rows[0].cells[2].text = 'Score'
for item in data:
    row = table.add_row().cells
    row[0].text = item['name']
    row[1].text = item['status']
    row[2].text = str(item['score'])

doc.save('report.docx')
```

Excel with formatting and auto-filter:
```python
from openpyxl import Workbook
from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment

wb = Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws.title = 'Results'

# Header row
headers = ['Host', 'Severity', 'CVE', 'Score']
header_fill = PatternFill('solid', fgColor='4472C4')
header_font = Font(bold=True, color='FFFFFF')
for col, h in enumerate(headers, 1):
    cell = ws.cell(row=1, column=col, value=h)
    cell.fill = header_fill
    cell.font = header_font

# Data rows
for row_data in data:
    ws.append([row_data[h] for h in headers])

ws.auto_filter.ref = ws.dimensions
ws.freeze_panes = 'A2'
for col in ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']:
    ws.column_dimensions[col].width = 20

wb.save('results.xlsx')
```

CSV export:
```python
import csv

with open('output.csv', 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=['host', 'severity', 'cve'])
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerows(data)
```

PDF with fpdf2:
```python
from fpdf import FPDF

pdf = FPDF()
pdf.set_auto_page_break(auto=True, margin=15)
pdf.add_page()
pdf.set_font('Helvetica', 'B', 16)
pdf.cell(text='Security Report', new_x='LMARGIN', new_y='NEXT')
pdf.set_font('Helvetica', size=11)
pdf.multi_cell(w=0, text='Report content here...')
pdf.output('report.pdf')
```
caveats: python-docx cannot open or modify .doc (old binary format) — only .docx
openpyxl cannot recalculate Excel formulas — if you write formulas, they only update when opened in Excel/LibreOffice
fpdf2 is the maintained fork of PyFPDF — install as 'fpdf2' (pip install fpdf2), import as 'from fpdf import FPDF'
For large datasets (>100K rows), consider writing CSV instead of xlsx to avoid memory issues
python-docx table style names are locale-sensitive — 'Light List Accent 1' works on English installs
